title: In 2022, beverage CO₂ prices spiked 50% in some regions. The same supply chain failure is still in place.
excerpt: A breakdown of what actually caused the shortage, why it keeps happening, and what distributed domestic capture looks like as a structural fix.

title: This is what domestic CO₂ supply looks like when you build it from scratch
excerpt: CleanCycleCarbon captures CO₂ off the backend of renewable natural gas upgraders and purifies it to beverage grade. We operate one of only two facilities in the US doing this.

title: Most people have no idea where the CO₂ in their beer comes from
excerpt: The supply chain is thin. A handful of ammonia plants, some ethanol facilities, a few natural gas processors. When one goes down, prices spike and allocations get cut.

title: The day we hit beverage grade
excerpt: We celebrated, then got back to work. Because hitting the spec once is a milestone. Hitting it every day is the business.
